estimatedMonthlySavings: ~$2-3 USD/month if Tier 2 Sonnet crons right-sized (2 crons × ~34 runs/month × ~$0.05/run savings)
notes: 10 systemEvent crons incur zero LLM cost. 10 agentTurn crons already on Haiku/Gemma/qwen3.5. 2 Sonnet agentTurns classified Tier 2 are pending-poc. 4 complex crons justified on Sonnet.

crons.name: AInchors Daily Backup
crons.rationale: Runs one script, reports PASS/FAIL. 1-2 tool calls. Already on Haiku — correctly placed.

crons.name: Google Workspace Setup Reminder
crons.rationale: systemEvent — no LLM invoked. Already expired/deleted.

crons.name: AInchors Daily Close — Journal
crons.rationale: systemEvent — runtime handles this without LLM invocation from cron layer. Journal writing is triggered in main session context.

crons.name: AInchors Morning Stand-Up
crons.rationale: 8-section stand-up: governance review (Shield/Lex/Sage/Warden), RTB framework analysis, framework maturity gate, multi-source data synthesis, Notion US creation, Telegram delivery. Multi-step judgment at scale — Sonnet required.

crons.name: AInchors Monthly Model Strategy Review
crons.rationale: Governance-level review: cost analysis, benchmark runs, drift review, policy change recommendations, Notion/HTML updates, Ken sign-off loop. High-stakes decision support — Sonnet required.

crons.name: AInchors Midday Cost Tracker
crons.rationale: Runs one script, outputs one-line summary. Already on Haiku — correctly placed.

crons.name: AInchors Weekly Asset Review
crons.rationale: Script run + review flagged assets + Notion update. Medium multi-step. Already on Gemma (free local model) — correctly placed and at zero API cost.

crons.name: AInchors Gateway Health Check (silent)
crons.rationale: systemEvent — no LLM invoked. Pure script execution.

crons.name: AInchors Quarterly Asset Registry Review
crons.rationale: Multi-step: script run, full asset registry review, structured quarterly report, Telegram to Ken, async reply handling (72h window), conditional Notion US creation. Judgment + async interaction flow — Sonnet required. Being conservative.

crons.name: AInchors Daily Backup (shell-direct, resilient)
crons.rationale: systemEvent — resilient backup path, no LLM dependency by design.

crons.name: AInchors Auto-Heal (nightly)
crons.rationale: systemEvent — runs auto-heal.sh directly. No LLM invoked from cron layer.

crons.name: Yoda → Aria daily context sync
crons.rationale: systemEvent — sync runs as systemEvent in main session context, no standalone LLM invocation from cron layer.

crons.name: Aria Daily Summary (business stream)
crons.rationale: Reviews session history, writes structured bullet-point summary to file. Output is structured and format-driven (not creative). Haiku can handle session summarisation to a fixed template. Daily cadence (30 runs/month) makes this a meaningful saving candidate.

crons.name: AInchors Mission Control Refresh
crons.rationale: systemEvent — runs shell script to regenerate dashboard data. Zero LLM cost.

crons.name: AInchors Daily Close — Blog
crons.rationale: Complex: reads journal, writes styled HTML blog post (600s timeout), PII sweep, spawns Lex subagent for governance gate, iterative fix loop, git commit. Creative quality + governance judgment — Sonnet required.

crons.name: Warden — Model Compliance Check
crons.rationale: Runs drift check script, logs result, writes escalation file on violations. Structured 3-step task. Already on Haiku — correctly placed. High frequency (96×/day) — Haiku essential.

crons.name: Shield 🛡️ — Security Review Sweep (daily)
crons.rationale: LLM-level security review with S1-S5 reasoning on pending assets. Requires some reasoning depth. Currently Haiku — acceptable for pattern-based security checks. Monitor output quality.

crons.name: Lex ⚖️ — Legal Review Sweep (daily)
crons.rationale: Legal reasoning over pending assets (L1-L5 checks). Requires reasoning. Currently Haiku — acceptable for rule-based legal flag checks on known patterns. Monitor for missed risks.

crons.name: Sage 🧪 — QA Review Sweep (daily)
crons.rationale: Structured QA checks (brief adherence, clarity, fact verification). Template-driven. Already on Haiku — correctly placed.

crons.name: AInchors Daily Burn Alert
crons.rationale: Read JSON, threshold check, conditional Telegram. 1-2 tool calls. Already on Haiku — correctly placed.

crons.name: AKB Daily Update — Obsidian + Notion
crons.rationale: Multi-step KB maintenance: read 4 files, detect changes, update 8+ Obsidian/Notion targets, git commit. Medium complexity. Already on qwen3.5:cloud (Ollama free tier) — zero API cost. Correctly placed.

crons.name: AInchors Fallback Chain Validation (boot + hourly)
crons.rationale: Run script, read state, conditional Telegram, output exactly one line. Minimal logic. Already on Haiku — correctly placed. 24×/day frequency makes Haiku essential.

crons.name: Aria 🔵 — Weekly Business ROI Summary
crons.rationale: Runs ROI script, reads 2 state files, composes plain-language summary in bullets, Telegram to Angie. Structured template output. Plain-language + data extraction is well within Haiku's capability. 4×/month — pending-poc validation recommended before switching.

crons.name: Aria→Ken Relay Queue Poller
crons.rationale: Read JSON, check count, relay or exit in one line. Minimal logic, high frequency (288×/day). Already on Haiku — critically important to keep on cheapest model.

crons.name: AInchors Monthly SLA Report
crons.rationale: systemEvent — script-based report generation. No LLM cost.

crons.name: AInchors Observability Collector
crons.rationale: systemEvent — runs obs-collector.sh silently. Zero LLM cost.

crons.name: AInchors Task Monitor
crons.rationale: systemEvent — runs task-collector.sh, checks alert JSON. Zero LLM cost.

crons.name: TRIGGER-04/06: OpenClaw Release Monitor
crons.rationale: Fetch GitHub API, compare versions, conditional Telegram alert. 2-3 tool calls. Already on Haiku — correctly placed.
